coastal.description:
An earthquake has been felt near Victoria, British Columbia. There are no reports of major damage or injuries and none are expected. More information is available from Natural Resources Canada. kamloops.title: Honour Ranch: Protecting a Place of Healing with FireSmart Principles
kamloops.description:
Located near Ashcroft, BC, Honour Ranch is a sanctuary dedicated to the mental health and well-being of veterans, military members, first responders, and emergency personnel. Operated by the Honour House Society, the Ranch provides a peaceful, restorative setting where front-line workers can access mental health support, peer connection, and recovery programming.
The post Honour Ranch: Protecting

kamloops.title: EXPIRED: Evacuation Order: Lower Similkameen Indian Band. Rockslide. Nov 6, 2023.
kamloops.description:
On November 6th, the Lower Similkameen Indian Band issued an Evacuation Order (PDF) due to imminent danger from a rockslide. The risk of danger has now passed. This Evacuation Order expired on November 10, 2023. Recommended Actions Follow directions from your Local Authority or First Nation. Resources Know the signs of landslide and be prepared. Visit […]

kamloops.title: Evacuation Alert due to Rockslide: Regional District of Okanagan-Similkameen. Electoral Area “F”. Aug. 30, 2023.
kamloops.description:
On August 30th, 2023, the Regional District of Okanagan-Similkameen issued an Evacuation Alert for four properties in Electoral Area “F” along North Beach Road, due to a rockslide. The Evacuation Alert has been issued as a precautionary measure while the province conducts geotechnical assessments. Anyone in the affected area must be ready to leave on […]

prince-george.title: Water Usage Restriction Order EXTENDED: Village of McBride. Severe Drought. Nov. 27, 2023.
prince-george.description:
The Village of McBride has extended the State of Local Emergency (PDF) to December 4, 2023 due to severe drought. The Water Usage Restriction Order (PDF) issued on September 19, 2023 remains in effect. all.title: Why Partnerships Are Critical to Building Wildfire Resiliency
all.description:
Since 2023, FireSmart BC has partnered with BCAA to strengthen wildfire resilience across British Columbia. This partnership focuses on expanding access to wildfire education and resources, especially in remote, rural, and high-risk areas.
The post Why Partnerships Are Critical to Building Wildfire Resiliency first appeared on FireSmart BC.
